title: Measurement-based quantum state transfer and restoring via spin-1/2 chain interacting with environment
authors: E. B. Fel'dman, A. I. Zenchuk
abstract: We consider the multi-qubit fixed-excitation state transfer along the spin chain with dipole-dipole interaction subjected to the interaction with environment governed by the Lindblad equation preserving the excitation number during spin-evolution. The state transfer algorithm includes the state restoring via Kraus operators and ancilla measurement. As a result, the transferred state appears in superposition with completely mixed state, the latter disappears with vanishing interaction with environment. In that case we deal with probabilistic perfect state transfer. Example of an arbitrary multi-qubit one-excitation state transfer is present and its robustness with respect to perturbation of the Kraus operators is studied.
summary: The paper proposes a method to recover pure multi-qubit states transferred through a noisy spin chain by using an enlarged receiver and ancilla measurements. It demonstrates that while environmental interaction introduces mixedness, the protocol can effectively restore the original state's structure. The method is shown to be robust against perturbations and can even outperform standard homogeneous chains when using engineered high-fidelity chains.
main_problem: Achieving high-fidelity transfer of multi-qubit k-excitation states along a spin chain while mitigating the degradation caused by environmental decoherence.
main_result: The authors develop a measurement-based algorithm using an extended receiver and ancilla measurement to restore the transferred state, achieving probabilistic perfect state transfer as environmental interaction vanishes.
method: The approach uses Kraus operators and a unitary transformation at an extended receiver, combined with an ancilla-based measurement strategy to remove 'garbage' terms from the density matrix.
model_or_system: A spin-1/2 chain with XX-type dipole-dipole interaction and an external magnetic field, evolving under a Lindblad master equation that preserves the excitation number.
key_observables: The lambda-parameter (state fidelity/proportionality), the nu-parameter (contribution of the maximally mixed state), and the success probability p.
important_parameters: Dimensionless interaction strength (Gamma/D12), dimensionless time (t*D12), and the number of nodes in the chain (N).
main_assumptions: The excitation number is conserved during spin evolution, and the state transfer is inherently probabilistic due to the measurement-based removal of error terms.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the communication line setup (Sender, TL, Receiver); Figure 2 depicts the quantum circuit for state transfer and restoration; Figure 3 displays the evolution of the lambda-parameter for various chain lengths.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of state transfer in noisy chains, defines the physical model and Lindblad dynamics, presents the state-restoring algorithm using Kraus operators, provides a dimensionality analysis for the extended receiver, and concludes with numerical simulations and robustness tests.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to open quantum systems and quantum networks as it provides a practical protocol for error mitigation and state recovery in noisy communication channels using local measurements.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
